title: quadrella indica
intro: Quadrella indica, also known as the "bitter apple," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Quadrella indica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and infusions.
